tools.name: create_contact
tools.description: Create a contact in the household's address book. Only firstName is required; all other details (address, date of birth, notes, a flag to prepare a birthday present) are optional and can be filled in later with update_contact.

tools.name: update_contact
tools.description: Update a contact by id. Only the fields supplied are changed; omitted fields keep their current value (dateOfBirth is the exception: an empty string explicitly clears it).

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Soft-delete a contact by id; it is marked deleted and no longer appears in list_contacts, but the underlying record is retained rather than permanently erased.

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List all contacts in the household. Takes no parameters; soft-deleted contacts are excluded.

tools.name: create_appliance
tools.description: Create an appliance record for the household, tracking purchase date, warranty expiry, and optional references to previously created warranty/insurance documents.

tools.name: update_appliance
tools.description: Update an appliance's details; omitted fields keep their current value. Rejects the update if the resulting warranty expiry date would fall before the purchase date.

tools.name: delete_appliance
tools.description: Soft-delete an appliance; it is hidden from list_appliances but not permanently erased.

tools.name: list_appliances
tools.description: List all appliances in the household

tools.name: create_calendar_event
tools.description: Creates a single, one-off calendar event on the household calendar. For a repeating schedule use create_recurring_calendar_event instead.

tools.name: update_calendar_event
tools.description: Updates fields on an existing single calendar event; only the fields supplied are changed, all others keep their current value. Imported (subscription-sourced) events cannot be edited.

tools.name: delete_calendar_event
tools.description: Soft-deletes a single calendar event by id. Events imported from a calendar subscription cannot be deleted here; unsubscribe from the source calendar instead.

tools.name: list_calendar_events
tools.description: List calendar events with optional date-range filters

tools.name: create_document
tools.description: Create a document (passport, insurance, certificate, warranty, license, other)

tools.name: update_document
tools.description: Update a document. Omitted fields unchanged.

tools.name: delete_document
tools.description: Soft-delete a document by id and cascade soft-delete its attachments; none of them appear in listings afterward, but the records are retained rather than permanently erased.

tools.name: list_documents
tools.description: List documents in the household, optionally filtered to a single document type. Soft-deleted documents are excluded.

tools.name: create_pet
tools.description: Create a pet. Supports microchip, vet contact, and insurance refs.

tools.name: update_pet
tools.description: Update a pet. Omitted fields unchanged.

tools.name: delete_pet
tools.description: Soft-delete a pet; it is hidden from list_pets but not permanently erased.

tools.name: list_pets
tools.description: List all pets in the household

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description: Create a subscription for the household to track a recurring cost, such as a streaming service or membership. Accepts an optional cron-based renewal schedule (must not repeat more frequently than once a day) and a fee amount.

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.description: Partially update an existing subscription; only the fields provided are changed, everything else is left as-is. For renewalCronExpression and notes, sending an empty string clears the stored value rather than leaving it unchanged.

tools.name: delete_subscription
tools.description: Soft-delete a subscription

tools.name: list_subscriptions
tools.description: List subscriptions with optional status filter

tools.name: create_vehicle
tools.description: Create a vehicle record for the household to track its details, registration and VIN, insurance document, and upcoming MOT/tax/service due dates. All fields are optional.

tools.name: update_vehicle
tools.description: Partially update an existing vehicle's details; only the fields provided are changed, everything else is left as-is. For string and due-date fields, omitting the field leaves the stored value unchanged, while sending an empty string clears it.

tools.name: delete_vehicle
tools.description: Soft-delete a vehicle

tools.name: list_vehicles
tools.description: List all vehicles in the household

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Creates a new household task. Only title is required; the task starts unassigned and uncompleted. Use assign_task, update_task, or complete_task to change it afterward.

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update task title, assignment, dates, or notes.

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Soft-deletes a task: it stops appearing in list_tasks but the record is retained rather than permanently removed. Fails if the task was already deleted.

tools.name: complete_task
tools.description: Marks a task as completed and records the completion for the household's momentum stats. Fails with a conflict if the task is already completed.

tools.name: uncomplete_task
tools.description: Reopens a completed task, clearing its completion timestamp and completed-by user. Fails with a conflict if the task is not currently completed.

tools.name: assign_task
tools.description: Assign or unassign a task to a household member.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: Lists tasks in the household with optional filters (assignee, due-date range, completion status) and cursor-based pagination. Excludes completed tasks by default; results are sorted by due date ascending, with tasks that have no due date listed first.

tools.name: get_weather_forecast
tools.description: Get weather forecast for the household's location

tools.name: get_house
tools.description: Return full details for the household the current session is scoped to: name, address, invite code, notes, join settings, members, subscription status, and resolved feature-flag states. No parameters — the household is resolved server-side from the authenticated session.

tools.name: list_house_members
tools.description: List household members with their userId, name, nickname, and role.

tools.name: list_houses
tools.description: List the households the current user belongs to (e.g. to tell the user which home you are acting on). The active house is determined server-side from the authenticated session.

tools.name: get_profile
tools.description: Return the authenticated user's profile: name, email, AI-consent status, and timezone. Also records this call as user activity (bumps last-seen) as a side effect. No parameters — the user is resolved server-side from the authenticated session.

tools.name: list_shopping_list_items
tools.description: List all shopping list items in the household

tools.name: add_shopping_list_item
tools.description: Add an item to the household's shopping list. A brand-new item gets a category auto-assigned from its name. If an unbought item with a matching name already exists, its quantity is merged into that item instead of creating a duplicate, keeping the existing item's category unchanged.

tools.name: buy_shopping_list_item
tools.description: Buy item (decrements quantity; removes at zero)

tools.name: get_house_review
tools.description: Return this week's pending House Review recommendations (data gaps like a missing appliance warranty date or vehicle MOT date), re-validated live so items already filled in, actioned, or superseded by an open task are dropped before they're returned. Pro-gated: fails if the household isn't on a Pro

tools.name: defer_recommendation
tools.description: Add Later: defer a House Review recommendation and create a 7-day task

tools.name: skip_recommendation
tools.description: Skip: permanently suppress a House Review recommendation

tools.name: store_memory
tools.description: Store a long-term memory about the household. Use sparingly for durable preferences, routines, constraints, or insights worth recalling in a future conversation. Recall first to avoid duplicates.

tools.name: update_memory
tools.description: Update an existing memory's name, description, or content. Type is immutable; to reclassify, forget and store again.

tools.name: forget_memory
tools.description: Forget (soft-delete) a memory by id. Use when a memory is superseded or no longer relevant.

tools.name: recall_memory
tools.description: Recall long-term memories stored about the current household. Call this at the start of advisory or open-ended turns. Filter by type when only one author's notes are relevant.

tools.name: create_meal_plan
tools.description: Plan a meal for a specific date and meal type. Provide recipeId to link an existing recipe (from list_recipes) OR name for a custom meal; when recipeId is given any name is ignored and stored as null.

tools.name: update_meal_plan
tools.description: Update a meal plan entry. Any field left out keeps its current value. Provide recipeId to link a recipe (clears any custom name) or name to make it a custom meal (clears the recipe link); recipeId takes precedence when both are given.

tools.name: delete_meal_plan
tools.description: Remove a planned meal from the household's meal plan. This is a soft delete: the entry stops appearing in list_meal_plans and its date/meal-type slot becomes free again.

tools.name: list_meal_plans
tools.description: List meal plans in the household with an optional date range

tools.name: create_recipe
tools.description: Create a new recipe in the household's recipe collection, with optional ingredients, source URL, instructions and serving size. Link it to a date with create_meal_plan, or browse it later with list_recipes.

tools.name: update_recipe
tools.description: Update an existing recipe. Only the fields provided are changed; any field left out keeps its current value. Providing ingredients replaces the entire ingredient list rather than merging with the existing one.

tools.name: delete_recipe
tools.description: Delete a recipe from the household's collection. This is a soft delete; any meal plan entries still linked to this recipe are automatically converted into custom entries that keep a snapshot of its name, ingredients and instructions, so they aren't left with a dangling recipe link.

tools.name: list_recipes
tools.description: List all recipes in the household

tools.name: list_global_recipes
tools.description: List the global/Discover recipe catalogue (curated recipes available to all households)

tools.name: batch_add_shopping_list_items
tools.description: Add multiple items to the shopping list at once

tools.name: update_shopping_list_item
tools.description: Update a shopping list item's name, quantity, and/or category. Only the fields provided are changed; any field left out keeps its current value.

tools.name: remove_shopping_list_item
tools.description: Remove an item from the household's shopping list without marking it as bought. This soft-deletes the item so it no longer appears in list_shopping_list_items.

tools.name: create_recurring_calendar_event
tools.description: Create a recurring calendar event that automatically generates event instances on a schedule.

tools.name: update_recurring_calendar_event
tools.description: Update a recurring calendar event. Changes to title/description/notes patch existing instances. Changes to frequency/dates/time regenerate future instances.

tools.name: delete_recurring_calendar_event
tools.description: Delete a recurring calendar event and all its future instances.

tools.name: create_recurring_task
tools.description: Create a recurring task that automatically generates task instances on a schedule.

tools.name: update_recurring_task
tools.description: Update a recurring task. Title/notes changes patch existing instances; frequency/date changes regenerate future instances.

tools.name: delete_recurring_task
tools.description: Delete a recurring task and all its uncompleted future instances.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.onehaus.app" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_profile, get_house_review, create_recipe · Private data: create_contact, update_contact, delete_contact, list_contacts, create_appliance, create_document, update_document, delete_document, list_documents, create_pet, create_vehicle, delete_task, complete_task, get_profile · Public sink: get_profile · Destructive: delete_contact, delete_appliance, delete_calendar_event, delete_document, delete_pet, delete_subscription, delete_vehicle, delete_task, forget_memory, delete_meal_plan, delete_recipe, remove_shopping_list_item, delete_recurring_calendar_event, delete_recurring_task.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_profile, get_house_review, create_recipe · Private data: create_contact, update_contact, delete_contact, list_contacts, create_appliance, create_document, update_document, delete_document, list_documents, create_pet, create_vehicle, delete_task, complete_task, get_profile · Public sink: get_profile · Destructive: delete_contact, delete_appliance, delete_calendar_event, delete_document, delete_pet, delete_subscription, delete_vehicle, delete_task, forget_memory, delete_meal_plan, delete_recipe, remove_shopping_list_item, delete_recurring_calendar_event, delete_recurring_task

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
